lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰ ਸਰਣਾਈ ਉਬਰੇ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਰ ਰਖਵਾਲਿਆ ॥੩੦॥
lines.translit: naanak gur saranaaee ubare har gur rakhavaaliaa |30|
lines.english: Nanak has come to the Sanctuary of the Guru, and is saved. The Guru, the Lord, is his Protector. ||30||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿਆ ਮਨ ਮੂਰਖ ਮਿਲੈ ਸਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: doojai bhaae naam visaariaa man moorakh milai sajaae |
lines.english: In the love of duality, they forget the Naam. Those foolish mortals shall receive their punishment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਪੰਡਿਤ ਵਾਦੁ ਵਖਾਣਦੇ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹ ਸੁਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: parr parr panddit vaad vakhaanade maaeaa moh suaae |
lines.english: Reading and writing, the Pandits engage in debates and disputes; they are attached to the flavors of Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੑਿ ਕੀਤੇ ਤਿਸੈ ਨ ਸੇਵਨੑੀ ਦੇਦਾ ਰਿਜਕੁ ਸਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jina keete tisai na sevanaee dedaa rijak samaae |
lines.english: They do not serve the One who created them, who gives sustenance to all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਮ ਕਾ ਫਾਹਾ ਗਲਹੁ ਨ ਕਟੀਐ ਫਿਰਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਆਵਹਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jam kaa faahaa galahu na katteeai fir fir aaveh jaae |
lines.english: The noose of Death around their necks is not cut off; they come and go in reincarnation, over and over again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਪੂਰਬਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਤਿਨ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: jin kau poorab likhiaa satigur miliaa tin aae |
lines.english: The True Guru comes and meets those who have such pre-ordained destiny.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇਦੇ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: anadin naam dhiaaeide naanak sach samaae |1|
lines.english: Night and day, they meditate on the Naam, the Name of the Lord; O Nanak, they merge into the True Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਵਣਜਹਿ ਸਚੁ ਸੇਵਦੇ ਜਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੈਰੀ ਪਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: sach vanajeh sach sevade ji guramukh pairee paeh |
lines.english: Those Gurmukhs who fall at His Feet deal with the True Lord and serve the True Lord.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਭਾਣੈ ਜੇ ਚਲਹਿ ਸਹਜੇ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਹਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak gur kai bhaanai je chaleh sahaje sach samaeh |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who walk in harmony with the Guru's Will are intuitively absorbed in the True Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਵਿਚਿ ਅਤਿ ਦੁਖੁ ਘਣਾ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa vich at dukh ghanaa manamukh chit laaeaa |
lines.english: In hope, there is very great pain; the self-willed manmukh focuses his consciousness on it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਭਏ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਪਰਮ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh bhe niraas param sukh paaeaa |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs become desireless, and attain supreme peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਚੇ ਗਿਰਹ ਉਦਾਸ ਅਲਿਪਤ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: viche girah udaas alipat liv laaeaa |
lines.english: In the midst of their household, they remain detached; they are lovingly attuned to the Detached Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਨਾ ਸੋਗੁ ਵਿਜੋਗੁ ਨ ਵਿਆਪਈ ਹਰਿ ਭਾਣਾ ਭਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: onaa sog vijog na viaapee har bhaanaa bhaaeaa |
lines.english: Sorrow and separation do not cling to them at all. They are pleased with the Lord's Will.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਸੇਤੀ ਸਦਾ ਰਵਿ ਰਹੇ ਧੁਰਿ ਲਏ ਮਿਲਾਇਆ ॥੩੧॥
lines.translit: naanak har setee sadaa rav rahe dhur le milaaeaa |31|
lines.english: O Nanak, they remain forever immersed in the Primal Lord, who blends them with Himself. ||31||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਗੁਰ ਥੈ ਟਿਕੈ ਹੋਰ ਥੈ ਪਰਗਟੁ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: gur kaa sabad gur thai ttikai hor thai paragatt na hoe |
lines.english: The Word of the Guru's Shabad rests in the Guru; it does not appear through anyone else.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਾਈ ਅਮਾਣ ਕਿਉ ਰਖੀਐ ਦਿਤੀ ਹੀ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: paraaee amaan kiau rakheeai ditee hee sukh hoe |
lines.english: Why keep what is held in trust for another? Giving it back, peace is found.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਨੑੇ ਵਸਿ ਮਾਣਕੁ ਪਇਆ ਘਰਿ ਘਰਿ ਵੇਚਣ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: anae vas maanak peaa ghar ghar vechan jaae |
lines.english: The blind man finds a jewel, and goes from house to house selling it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਨਾ ਪਰਖ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਅਢੁ ਨ ਪਲੈ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: onaa parakh na aavee adt na palai paae |
lines.english: But they cannot appraise it, and they do not offer him even half a shell for it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਓਸੁ ਨਾਲਿ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਏ ਤਾਂ ਵਥੁ ਲਹੈ ਨਉ ਨਿਧਿ ਪਲੈ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: je os naal chit laae taan vath lahai nau nidh palai paae |
lines.english: If he focuses his consciousness, then he obtains the true object, and he is blessed with the nine treasures.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਆਪਿ ਪਰਖ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਤਾਂ ਪਾਰਖੀਆ ਥਾਵਹੁ ਲਇਓੁ ਪਰਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: je aap parakh na aavee taan paarakheea thaavahu leo parakhaae |
lines.english: If he cannot appraise it himself, then he should have it appraised by an appraiser.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰਿ ਹੋਦੈ ਧਨਿ ਜਗੁ ਭੁਖਾ ਮੁਆ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸੋਝੀ ਨ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: ghar hodai dhan jag bhukhaa muaa bin satigur sojhee na hoe |
lines.english: The wealth is within the house, while the world is dying of hunger. Without the True Guru, no one has a clue.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦੁ ਸੀਤਲੁ ਮਨਿ ਤਨਿ ਵਸੈ ਤਿਥੈ ਸੋਗੁ ਵਿਜੋਗੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: sabad seetal man tan vasai tithai sog vijog na koe |
lines.english: When the cooling and soothing Shabad comes to dwell in the mind and body, there is no sorrow or separation there.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਬਿਨੁ ਬੂਝੇ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਪਾਇਓ ਫਿਰਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਆਵੈ ਜਾਏ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak bin boojhe kinai na paaeo fir fir aavai jaae |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, without understanding, no one obtains it; they come and go in reincarnation, over and over again. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਸਤੁ ਪਰਾਈ ਆਪਿ ਗਰਬੁ ਕਰੇ ਮੂਰਖੁ ਆਪੁ ਗਣਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: vasat paraaee aap garab kare moorakh aap ganaae |
lines.english: The object belongs to someone else, but the fool is proud of it, and shows his shallow nature.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਿ ਅਨਦੁ ਭਇਆ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਹਰਿ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮੁ ਸਰਸੇ ਸਜਣ ਸੰਤ ਪਿਆਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: man anad bheaa miliaa har preetam sarase sajan sant piaare |
lines.english: My mind is in ecstasy; I have met my Beloved Lord. My beloved friends, the Saints, are delighted.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਧੁਰਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਨ ਵਿਛੁੜਹਿ ਕਬਹੂ ਜਿ ਆਪਿ ਮੇਲੇ ਕਰਤਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: jo dhur mile na vichhurreh kabahoo ji aap mele karataare |
lines.english: Those who are united with the Primal Lord shall never be separated again. The Creator has united them with Himself.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਰਵਿਆ ਗੁਰੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਸਗਲੇ ਦੂਖ ਨਿਵਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: antar sabad raviaa gur paaeaa sagale dookh nivaare |
lines.english: The Shabad permeates my inner being, and I have found the Guru; all my sorrows are dispelled.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸੁਖਦਾਤਾ ਸਦਾ ਸਲਾਹੀ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਰਖਾਂ ਉਰ ਧਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: har sukhadaataa sadaa salaahee antar rakhaan ur dhaare |
lines.english: I praise forever the Lord, the Giver of peace; I keep Him enshrined deep within my heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖੁ ਤਿਨ ਕੀ ਬਖੀਲੀ ਕਿ ਕਰੇ ਜਿ ਸਚੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਵਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh tin kee bakheelee ki kare ji sachai sabad savaare |
lines.english: How can the self-willed manmukh gossip about those who are embellished and exalted in the True Word of the Shabad?

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਨਾ ਦੀ ਆਪਿ ਪਤਿ ਰਖਸੀ ਮੇਰਾ ਪਿਆਰਾ ਸਰਣਾਗਤਿ ਪਏ ਗੁਰ ਦੁਆਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: onaa dee aap pat rakhasee meraa piaaraa saranaagat pe gur duaare |
lines.english: My Beloved Himself preserves the honor of those who have come to the Guru's Door seeking Sanctuary.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸਤਰੀ ਪੁਰਖੈ ਬਹੁ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਮੋਹੁ ਵਧਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: eisataree purakhai bahu preet mil mohu vadhaaeaa |
lines.english: The husband and wife are very much in love; joining together, their love increases.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੇ ਸੁਹੇਲੇ ਭਏ ਮੁਖ ਊਜਲ ਦਰਬਾਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh se suhele bhe mukh aoojal darabaare |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukhs are filled with joy; their faces are radiant in the Court of the Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਤ੍ਰੁ ਕਲਤ੍ਰੁ ਨਿਤ ਵੇਖੈ ਵਿਗਸੈ ਮੋਹਿ ਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: putru kalatru nit vekhai vigasai mohi maaeaa |
lines.english: Gazing on his children and his wife, the man is pleased and attached to Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਸਿ ਪਰਦੇਸਿ ਧਨੁ ਚੋਰਾਇ ਆਣਿ ਮੁਹਿ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: des parades dhan choraae aan muhi paaeaa |
lines.english: Stealing the wealth of his own country and other lands, he brings it home and feeds them.
